 18 | class EyeCareService : AccessibilityService() {

 39 |     enum class GuardianState {

 41 |         SCANNING,   // Shield UP, analyzing screen
 42 |         ARMED,      // Target acquired (Eyecare + On detected)
 43 |         FIRING      // Active countermeasures
 44 |     }

 46 |     // SCREEN RECEIVER
 47 |     private val screenReceiver = object : BroadcastReceiver() {
 48 |         override fun onReceive(context: android.content.Context, intent: Intent) {
 49 |             if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF) {
 50 |                 isScreenActive = false
 51 |                 TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs = System.currentTimeMillis()
 52 |                 TimeManager.save(context)
 53 |                 hideShield() // Safety drop
 54 |             } else if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON) {
 55 |                 isScreenActive = true
 56 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
 57 |                 if (TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs > 0 && (now - TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs) > 15 * 60 * 1000L) {
 58 |                     TimeManager.resetContinuous(context)
 59 |                     DebugLogger.log("TIMER", "Strict rest period completed. Usage reset.")
 60 |                 }
 61 |                 TimeManager.sessionStart = now
 62 |             }
 63 |         }
 64 |     }

 66 |     override fun onServiceConnected() {
 67 |         super.onServiceConnected() // Important
 68 |         startForegroundService()
 69 |         StatsManager.init(this)
 70 |         TimeManager.init(this)
 71 |         windowManager = getSystemService(WindowManager::class.java)
 72 |         TimeManager.sessionStart = System.currentTimeMillis()
 73 | 
 74 |         val filter = IntentFilter().apply {
 75 |             addAction(Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON)
 76 |             addAction(Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF)
 77 |         }
 78 |         registerReceiver(screenReceiver, filter)

 80 |         runGuardianLoop()
 81 |         DebugLogger.log("INIT", "Guardian Service Started. Monitoring...")
 82 |     }

 84 |     private fun startForegroundService() {
 85 |         val channelId = "eyecare_min_channel"
 86 |         val nm = getSystemService(android.app.NotificationManager::class.java)
 87 |         if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
 88 |             val chan = android.app.NotificationChannel(
 89 |                 channelId,
 90 |                 "Monitor",
 91 |                 android.app.NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_MIN
 92 |             )
 93 |             chan.setShowBadge(false)
 94 |             nm.createNotificationChannel(chan)
 95 |         }
 96 |         val notif = androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.Builder(this, channelId)
 97 |             .setContentTitle("Eyecare")
 98 |             .setContentText("Active")
 99 |             .setSmallIcon(android.R.drawable.ic_menu_view)
100 |             .setPriority(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_MIN)
101 |             .setCategory(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.CATEGORY_SERVICE)
102 |             .setOngoing(true)
103 |             .build()
104 |         startForeground(2024, notif)
105 |     }

107 |     override fun onAccessibilityEvent(event: AccessibilityEvent?) {
108 |         if (event == null) return
109 |         val pkg = event.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
110 |         val className = event.className?.toString() ?: ""
111 |         val type = event.eventType
112 | 
113 |         if (pkg == packageName) return
114 |         if (NukeManager.isProtectionDisabled(this)) return
115 | 
116 |         // 1. GLOBAL STATE TRACKING
117 |         if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGED) {
118 |             currentPackage = pkg
119 |             lastWindowChangeTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
120 |             DebugLogger.log("WINDOW", "Changed: $pkg / $className")
121 |             
122 |             // RESET State on Window Change (Unless ARMED, which persists one hop)
123 |             if (guardianState == GuardianState.FIRING) {
124 |                 // Keep firing
125 |             } else if (guardianState == GuardianState.ARMED) {
126 |                  // We stay ARMED for the transition to the toggle screen
127 |                  DebugLogger.log("STATE", "Window Changed while ARMED. Watching for speed entry...")
128 |             } else {
129 |                 // Default Reset
130 |                 guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
131 |             }
132 | 
133 |             // DANGER ZONE DETECTION
134 |             if (pkg.contains("settings") || pkg.contains("accessibility") || pkg.contains("packageinstaller")) {
135 |                 
136 |                 // START TACTICAL SHIELD
137 |                 // We only shield if we are not already firing
138 |                 if (guardianState != GuardianState.FIRING) {
139 |                     showShield() // FREEZE USER (2s Max)
140 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.SCANNING
141 |                     DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Shield UP. Scanning for Anchors in $pkg...")
142 |                 }
143 | 
144 |                 wasInDangerZone = true
145 |             } else {
146 |                 wasInDangerZone = false
147 |                 hideShield() // Safe zone, drop shield
148 |             }
149 |             
150 |             verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = false
151 |         }
152 | 
153 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
154 | 
155 |         // 2. SCANNING & ARMING LOGIC (While Shield is UP)
156 |         if (guardianState == GuardianState.SCANNING) {
157 |             val textList = mutableListOf<String>()
158 |             collectText(root, textList)
159 |             
160 |             // ANCHOR LOGIC: Find "Eyecare" followed closely by "On"
161 |             // Also look for "Installed apps" or "Downloaded apps" context if possible, but prioritize Eyecare+On
162 |             
163 |             val eyecareIndex = textList.indexOfFirst { it.contains("Eyecare", ignoreCase = true) }
164 |             
165 |             if (eyecareIndex != -1) {
166 |                 DebugLogger.log("SCAN", "Found Anchor 'Eyecare' at index $eyecareIndex")
167 |                 
168 |                 // Check proximity (next 2 nodes)
169 |                 var foundOn = false
170 |                 val maxSearch = (eyecareIndex + 3).coerceAtMost(textList.size)
171 |                 for (i in (eyecareIndex + 1) until maxSearch) {
172 |                     if (textList[i].equals("On", ignoreCase = true) || textList[i].contains("On", ignoreCase = true)) {
173 |                         foundOn = true
174 |                         break
175 |                     }
176 |                 }
177 | 
178 |                 if (foundOn) {
179 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.ARMED
180 |                     DebugLogger.log("STATE", "*** TARGET ACQUIRED *** -> ARMED. 'Eyecare' + 'On' detected.")
181 |                     hideShield() // Drop shield to allow user to proceed (into our trap)
182 |                 } else {
183 |                     // Found Eyecare, but it says "Off" or nothing. Safe.
184 |                     DebugLogger.log("SCAN", "Found 'Eyecare' but no 'On'. Safe/Inactive.")
185 |                     hideShield()
186 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
187 |                 }
188 |             } else {
189 |                 // No Eyecare text found yet. Keep shield up until timeout or next event.
190 |             }
191 |         }
192 | 
193 |         // 3. FIRING LOGIC (Speed Trap)
194 |         // Logic: If ARMED, and we detect a touch, check time since window load.
195 |         if (guardianState == GuardianState.ARMED) {
196 |             
197 |             if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_VIEW_CLICKED) {
198 |                 val timeSinceLoad = System.currentTimeMillis() - lastWindowChangeTime
199 |                 DebugLogger.log("TRAP", "Click detected. Time delta: \${timeSinceLoad}ms")
200 |                 
201 |                 // 1000ms Speed Limit for ARMED targets
202 |                 if (timeSinceLoad < 1000) {
203 |                     DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "*** SPEED TRAP TRIGGERED *** (Delta: $timeSinceLoad < 1000ms)")
204 |                     machineGun()
205 |                     return
206 |                 } else {
207 |                     // User waited long enough. 
208 |                     // But wait! If they are clicking the Toggle now, we need to fallback to the Trinity Check.
209 |                     // We don't disarm just yet, we let the Trinity check below handle the final toggle click.
210 |                 }
211 |             }
212 |         }
213 | 
214 |         // 4. LEGACY LAYERS (The Safety Nets)
215 | 
216 |         // A. THE TRINITY CHECK (Specific Toggle Screen Protection)
217 |         // This catches them if they pass the Speed Trap but try to toggle OFF.
218 |         if (pkg.contains("settings")) {
219 |             val hasName = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Eyecare").isNotEmpty()
220 |             if (hasName) {
221 |                 val descriptionText = getString(R.string.accessibility_desc)
222 |                 val hasDescription = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(descriptionText).isNotEmpty()
223 |                 
224 |                 if (hasDescription) {
225 |                     val switchNode = findSwitch(root)
226 |                     val isChecked = switchNode?.isChecked == true
227 | 
228 |                     if (isChecked) {
229 |                         // Trinity Complete: Name + Desc + ON -> User is trying to Turn OFF.
230 |                         DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "Trinity Violation: Attempt to disable service.")
231 |                         machineGun()
232 |                         return
233 |                     }
234 |                 }
235 |             }
236 |         }
237 | 
238 |         // B. APP INFO FREEZE (Uninstall Protection)
239 |         val hasUninstall = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Uninstall").isNotEmpty()
240 |         val hasForceStop = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Force stop").isNotEmpty() || 
241 |                            root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("FORCE STOP").isNotEmpty()
242 |         
243 |         if (hasUninstall && hasForceStop) {
244 |             val hasAppName = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Eyecare").isNotEmpty()
245 |             if (hasAppName) {
246 |                  DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "App Info Detected for Eyecare. KILLING.")
247 |                  machineGun()
248 |                  startTripwire()
249 |                  return
250 |             }
251 | 
252 |             // Generic App Info Logic (Freeze)
253 |             if (!verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
254 |                 val isSafe = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Permissions").isNotEmpty() || 
255 |                              root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Notifications").isNotEmpty()
256 |                 if (isSafe) {
257 |                     verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = true
258 |                 } else {
259 |                     if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_VIEW_CLICKED) {
260 |                         DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "Freeze Violation: Interaction on unverified page.")
261 |                         machineGun()
262 |                     }
263 |                 }
264 |             }
265 |         }
266 | 
267 |         // C. DIALOG TRAP
268 |         if (className.contains("Dialog") || className.contains("popup", ignoreCase = true)) {
269 |              val sb = StringBuilder()
270 |              collectTextRecursive(root, sb)
271 |              val text = sb.toString()
272 |              if (text.contains("Eyecare", ignoreCase = true) && 
273 |                 (text.contains("Stop", ignoreCase = true) || text.contains("Turn off", ignoreCase = true))) {
274 |                  DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "Dialog Trap: Destructive text detected.")
275 |                  machineGun()
276 |              }
277 |         }
278 |     }

280 |     // --- TACTICAL SHIELD IMPLEMENTATION ---
281 |     
282 |     private fun showShield() {
283 |         if (shieldView != null) return // Already UP
284 |         if (!android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(this)) return
285 | 
286 |         try {
287 |             val params = WindowManager.LayoutParams(
288 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
289 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
290 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY,
291 |                 // FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE = Allow key events to pass behind (like Back btn), 
292 |                 // BUT we do NOT set FLAG_NOT_TOUCH_MODAL. This means we CONSUME touches.
293 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE or 
294 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_LAYOUT_IN_SCREEN,
295 |                 PixelFormat.TRANSLUCENT
296 |             )
297 |             params.gravity = Gravity.CENTER
298 | 
299 |             shieldView = FrameLayout(this).apply {
300 |                 setBackgroundColor(Color.TRANSPARENT) // Invisible
301 |                 // Optional: setBackgroundColor(Color.parseColor("#22FF0000")) // Debug Red Tint
302 |             }
303 | 
304 |             windowManager?.addView(shieldView, params)
305 |             // DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Visual Shield Deployed")
306 | 
307 |             // AUTO-DISMISS TIMER (2s MAX)
308 |             shieldJob?.cancel()
309 |             shieldJob = scope.launch {
310 |                 delay(2000)
311 |                 if (guardianState == GuardianState.SCANNING) {
312 |                     DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Timeout: No anchors found. Dropping shield.")
313 |                     hideShield()
314 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
315 |                 }
316 |             }
317 | 
318 |         } catch (e: Exception) {
319 |             DebugLogger.log("ERROR", "Failed to deploy shield: \${e.message}")
320 |         }
321 |     }
322 | 
323 |     private fun hideShield() {
324 |         if (shieldView == null) return
325 |         try {
326 |             windowManager?.removeView(shieldView)
327 |             shieldView = null
328 |             // DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Shield Retracted")
329 |         } catch (e: Exception) {
330 |             shieldView = null
331 |         }
332 |     }
333 | 
334 |     // --- UTILS ---
335 | 
336 |     private fun collectText(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, list: MutableList<String>) {
337 |         if (node == null) return
338 |         if (node.text != null && node.text.isNotEmpty()) list.add(node.text.toString())
339 |         if (node.contentDescription != null && node.contentDescription.isNotEmpty()) list.add(node.contentDescription.toString())
340 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
341 |             collectText(node.getChild(i), list)
342 |         }
343 |     }
344 | 
345 |     private fun collectTextRecursive(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, sb: StringBuilder) {
346 |         if (node == null) return
347 |         if (node.text != null) sb.append(node.text).append(" ")
348 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
349 |             collectTextRecursive(node.getChild(i), sb)
350 |         }
351 |     }
352 | 
353 |     private fun findSwitch(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?): AccessibilityNodeInfo? {
354 |         if (node == null) return null
355 |         if (node.className == "android.widget.Switch") return node
356 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
357 |             val result = findSwitch(node.getChild(i))
358 |             if (result != null) return result
359 |         }
360 |         return null
361 |     }
362 | 
363 |     private fun machineGun() {
364 |         guardianState = GuardianState.FIRING
365 |         hideShield() // Drop shield so back button works
366 |         DebugLogger.log("COMBAT", "!!! MACHINE GUN FIRED !!!")
367 |         repeat(4) {
368 |             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
369 |         }
370 |         // Reset state after firing sequence
371 |         scope.launch {
372 |              delay(1000)
373 |              guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
374 |         }
375 |     }
376 | 
377 |     private fun startTripwire() {
378 |         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
379 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
380 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
381 |         i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "SECURITY_TRIPWIRE")
382 |         startActivity(i)
383 |     }
384 | 
385 |     private fun runGuardianLoop() {
386 |         timerJob = scope.launch {
387 |             while (isActive) {
388 |                 if (isScreenActive) {
389 |                     TimeManager.checkNewDay(this@EyeCareService)
390 | 
391 |                     if (!android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(applicationContext)) {
392 |                          val i = Intent(android.provider.Settings.ACTION_MANAGE_OVERLAY_PERMISSION)
393 |                          i.data = android.net.Uri.parse("package:$packageName")
394 |                          i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
395 |                          startActivity(i)
396 |                     }
397 | 
398 |                     if (TimeManager.isBreakActive) {
399 |                         val pkg = currentPackage
400 |                         val isSafe = isCriticalAppForeground()
401 |                         if (!isSafe && pkg != packageName) {
402 |                             DebugLogger.log("LEASH", "Escaped to $pkg. Yanks back.")
403 |                             val i = Intent(applicationContext, BreakActivity::class.java)
404 |                             i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
405 |                             i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT)
406 |                             i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
407 |                             startActivity(i)
408 |                         }
409 |                     }
410 | 
411 |                     updateUsage()
412 |                     checkRules()
413 |                     StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
414 | 
415 |                     if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs % 60_000L < 2500) {
416 |                         TimeManager.save(this@EyeCareService)
417 |                     }
418 |                     delay(1000)
419 |                 } else {
420 |                     delay(10000)
421 |                 }
422 |             }
423 |         }
424 |     }
425 | 
426 |     private fun isCriticalAppForeground(): Boolean {
427 |         val safeApps = setOf(
428 |             "com.google.android.apps.maps", "com.waze", 
429 |             "com.google.android.dialer", "com.android.phone", "com.android.server.telecom",
430 |             "com.google.android.deskclock", "com.sec.android.app.clockpackage", "com.android.deskclock"
431 |         )
432 |         val lowerPkg = currentPackage.lowercase()
433 |         if (safeApps.contains(currentPackage)) return true
434 |         return lowerPkg.contains("dialer") || lowerPkg.contains("call") || 
435 |                lowerPkg.contains("clock") || lowerPkg.contains("alarm")
436 |     }
437 | 
438 |     private fun updateUsage() {
439 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
440 |         val diff = now - TimeManager.sessionStart
441 |         TimeManager.sessionStart = now
442 |         TimeManager.continuousUsage += diff
443 |         TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs += diff
444 |     }
445 | 
446 |     private suspend fun checkRules() {
447 |         if (isCriticalAppForeground()) {
448 |              hideBanner()
449 |              return
450 |         }
451 | 
452 |         val h = java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
453 |         val isNightShift = (h >= 23 || h < 5)
454 |         if (isNightShift && TimeManager.isNightPassScheduled) {
455 |              hideBanner()
456 |              return 
457 |         }
458 | 
459 |         if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs >= TimeManager.DAILY_CAP) {
460 |              triggerBreak("DAILY_LIMIT", 0)
461 |              return
462 |         }
463 | 
464 |         val remainingLockout = TimeManager.getLockoutRemaining(this)
465 |         if (remainingLockout > 0) {
466 |              triggerBreak("MANUAL_LOCKOUT", remainingLockout)
467 |              return
468 |         }
469 | 
470 |         if (System.currentTimeMillis() < TimeManager.snoozeUntil) {
471 |              hideBanner()
472 |              return
473 |         }
474 | 
475 |         val usage = TimeManager.continuousUsage
476 | 
477 |         if (isNightShift) {
478 |              hideBanner()
479 |              triggerBreak("SLEEP_MODE", 0)
480 |              return
481 |         }
482 |         
483 |         if (TimeManager.nextLongBreak - usage <= 0) {
484 |              triggerBreak("EXTENDED", 50 * 60 * 1000L)
485 |              TimeManager.nextLongBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_2_HOUR
486 |              return
487 |         }
488 |         if (TimeManager.nextMediumBreak - usage <= 0) {
489 |              triggerBreak("LONG", 20 * 60 * 1000L)
490 |              TimeManager.nextMediumBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_60_MIN
491 |              return
492 |         }
493 |         if (TimeManager.nextShortBreak - usage <= 0) {
494 |              triggerBreak("SHORT", 300 * 1000L)
495 |              TimeManager.nextShortBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_30_MIN
496 |              return
497 |         }
498 |     }
499 | 
500 |     private fun triggerBreak(type: String, duration: Long) {
501 |         if (currentPackage == packageName && TimeManager.isBreakActive) return
502 |         val i = Intent(this, BreakActivity::class.java)
503 |         i.putExtra("TYPE", type)
504 |         i.putExtra("DURATION", duration)
505 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT)
506 |         startActivity(i)
507 |     }
508 | 
509 |     private fun hideBanner() {
510 |         try {
511 |             if (warningView != null) {
512 |                 val container = warningView?.tag as? View
513 |                 if (container != null && container.parent != null) {
514 |                     windowManager?.removeView(container)
515 |                 }
516 |                 warningView = null
517 |             }
518 |         } catch (e: Exception) { }
519 |     }
520 | 
521 |     override fun onInterrupt() {
522 |         hideShield()
523 |     }
524 | 
525 |     override fun onDestroy() {
526 |         super.onDestroy()
527 |         hideShield()
528 |         try { unregisterReceiver(screenReceiver) } catch (e: Exception) {}
529 |     }
530 | }

 34 |

 37 |     private var wasInDangerZone = false
 38 | 
 39 |     enum class GuardianState {
 40 |         IDLE,       // Normal operation
 41 |         SCANNING,   // Shield UP, analyzing screen
 42 |         ARMED,      // Target acquired (Eyecare + On detected)
 43 |         FIRING      // Active countermeasures
 44 |     }
 45 | 
 46 |     // SCREEN RECEIVER
 47 |     private val screenReceiver = object : BroadcastReceiver() {
 48 |         override fun onReceive(context: android.content.Context, intent: Intent) {
 49 |             if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF) {
 50 |                 isScreenActive = false
 51 |                 TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs = System.currentTimeMillis()
 52 |                 TimeManager.save(context)
 53 |                 hideShield() // Safety drop
 54 |             } else if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON) {
 55 |                 isScreenActive = true
 56 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
 57 |                 if (TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs > 0 && (now - TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs) > 15 * 60 * 1000L) {
 58 |                     TimeManager.resetContinuous(context)
 59 |                     DebugLogger.log("TIMER", "Strict rest period completed. Usage reset.")
 60 |                 }
 61 |                 TimeManager.sessionStart = now
 62 |             }
 63 |         }
 64 |     }
 65 | 
 66 |     override fun onServiceConnected() {
 67 |         super.onServiceConnected() // Important
 68 |         startForegroundService()
 69 |         StatsManager.init(this)
 70 |         TimeManager.init(this)
 71 |         windowManager = getSystemService(WindowManager::class.java)
 72 |         TimeManager.sessionStart = System.currentTimeMillis()
 73 | 
 74 |         val filter = IntentFilter().apply {
 75 |             addAction(Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON)
 76 |             addAction(Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF)
 77 |         }
 78 |         registerReceiver(screenReceiver, filter)

 80 |         runGuardianLoop()
 81 |         DebugLogger.log("INIT", "Guardian Service Started. Monitoring...")
 82 |     }

 84 |     private fun startForegroundService() {
 85 |         val channelId = "eyecare_min_channel"
 86 |         val nm = getSystemService(android.app.NotificationManager::class.java)
 87 |         if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
 88 |             val chan = android.app.NotificationChannel(
 89 |                 channelId,
 90 |                 "Monitor",
 91 |                 android.app.NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_MIN
 92 |             )
 93 |             chan.setShowBadge(false)
 94 |             nm.createNotificationChannel(chan)
 95 |         }
 96 |         val notif = androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.Builder(this, channelId)
 97 |             .setContentTitle("Eyecare")
 98 |             .setContentText("Active")
 99 |             .setSmallIcon(android.R.drawable.ic_menu_view)
100 |             .setPriority(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_MIN)
101 |             .setCategory(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.CATEGORY_SERVICE)
102 |             .setOngoing(true)
103 |             .build()
104 |         startForeground(2024, notif)
105 |     }

107 |     override fun onAccessibilityEvent(event: AccessibilityEvent?) {
108 |         if (event == null) return
109 |         val pkg = event.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
110 |         val className = event.className?.toString() ?: ""
111 |         val type = event.eventType
112 | 
113 |         if (pkg == packageName) return
114 |         if (NukeManager.isProtectionDisabled(this)) return
115 | 
116 |         // 1. GLOBAL STATE TRACKING
117 |         if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGED) {
118 |             currentPackage = pkg
119 |             lastWindowChangeTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
120 |             DebugLogger.log("WINDOW", "Changed: $pkg / $className")
121 |             
122 |             // RESET State on Window Change (Unless ARMED, which persists one hop)
123 |             if (guardianState == GuardianState.FIRING) {
124 |                 // Keep firing
125 |             } else if (guardianState == GuardianState.ARMED) {
126 |                  // We stay ARMED for the transition to the toggle screen
127 |                  DebugLogger.log("STATE", "Window Changed while ARMED. Watching for speed entry...")
128 |             } else {
129 |                 // Default Reset
130 |                 guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
131 |             }
132 | 
133 |             // DANGER ZONE DETECTION
134 |             if (pkg.contains("settings") || pkg.contains("accessibility") || pkg.contains("packageinstaller")) {
135 |                 
136 |                 // START TACTICAL SHIELD
137 |                 // We only shield if we are not already firing
138 |                 if (guardianState != GuardianState.FIRING) {
139 |                     showShield() // FREEZE USER (2s Max)
140 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.SCANNING
141 |                     DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Shield UP. Scanning for Anchors in $pkg...")
142 |                 }
143 | 
144 |                 wasInDangerZone = true
145 |             } else {
146 |                 wasInDangerZone = false
147 |                 hideShield() // Safe zone, drop shield
148 |             }
149 |             
150 |             verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = false
151 |         }
152 | 
153 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
154 | 
155 |         // 2. SCANNING & ARMING LOGIC (While Shield is UP)
156 |         if (guardianState == GuardianState.SCANNING) {
157 |             val textList = mutableListOf<String>()
158 |             collectText(root, textList)
159 |             
160 |             // ANCHOR LOGIC: Find "Eyecare" followed closely by "On"
161 |             // Also look for "Installed apps" or "Downloaded apps" context if possible, but prioritize Eyecare+On
162 |             
163 |             val eyecareIndex = textList.indexOfFirst { it.contains("Eyecare", ignoreCase = true) }
164 |             
165 |             if (eyecareIndex != -1) {
166 |                 DebugLogger.log("SCAN", "Found Anchor 'Eyecare' at index $eyecareIndex")
167 |                 
168 |                 // Check proximity (next 2 nodes)
169 |                 var foundOn = false
170 |                 val maxSearch = (eyecareIndex + 3).coerceAtMost(textList.size)
171 |                 for (i in (eyecareIndex + 1) until maxSearch) {
172 |                     if (textList[i].equals("On", ignoreCase = true) || textList[i].contains("On", ignoreCase = true)) {
173 |                         foundOn = true
174 |                         break
175 |                     }
176 |                 }
177 | 
178 |                 if (foundOn) {
179 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.ARMED
180 |                     DebugLogger.log("STATE", "*** TARGET ACQUIRED *** -> ARMED. 'Eyecare' + 'On' detected.")
181 |                     hideShield() // Drop shield to allow user to proceed (into our trap)
182 |                 } else {
183 |                     // Found Eyecare, but it says "Off" or nothing. Safe.
184 |                     DebugLogger.log("SCAN", "Found 'Eyecare' but no 'On'. Safe/Inactive.")
185 |                     hideShield()
186 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
187 |                 }
188 |             } else {
189 |                 // No Eyecare text found yet. Keep shield up until timeout or next event.
190 |             }
191 |         }
192 | 
193 |         // 3. FIRING LOGIC (Speed Trap)
194 |         // Logic: If ARMED, and we detect a touch, check time since window load.
195 |         if (guardianState == GuardianState.ARMED) {
196 |             
197 |             if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_VIEW_CLICKED) {
198 |                 val timeSinceLoad = System.currentTimeMillis() - lastWindowChangeTime
199 |                 DebugLogger.log("TRAP", "Click detected. Time delta: \${timeSinceLoad}ms")
200 |                 
201 |                 // 1000ms Speed Limit for ARMED targets
202 |                 if (timeSinceLoad < 1000) {
203 |                     DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "*** SPEED TRAP TRIGGERED *** (Delta: $timeSinceLoad < 1000ms)")
204 |                     machineGun()
205 |                     return
206 |                 } else {
207 |                     // User waited long enough. 
208 |                     // But wait! If they are clicking the Toggle now, we need to fallback to the Trinity Check.
209 |                     // We don't disarm just yet, we let the Trinity check below handle the final toggle click.
210 |                 }
211 |             }
212 |         }
213 | 
214 |         // 4. LEGACY LAYERS (The Safety Nets)
215 | 
216 |         // A. THE TRINITY CHECK (Specific Toggle Screen Protection)
217 |         // This catches them if they pass the Speed Trap but try to toggle OFF.
218 |         if (pkg.contains("settings")) {
219 |             val hasName = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Eyecare").isNotEmpty()
220 |             if (hasName) {
221 |                 val descriptionText = getString(R.string.accessibility_desc)
222 |                 val hasDescription = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(descriptionText).isNotEmpty()
223 |                 
224 |                 if (hasDescription) {
225 |                     val switchNode = findSwitch(root)
226 |                     val isChecked = switchNode?.isChecked == true
227 | 
228 |                     if (isChecked) {
229 |                         // Trinity Complete: Name + Desc + ON -> User is trying to Turn OFF.
230 |                         DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "Trinity Violation: Attempt to disable service.")
231 |                         machineGun()
232 |                         return
233 |                     }
234 |                 }
235 |             }
236 |         }
237 | 
238 |         // B. APP INFO FREEZE (Uninstall Protection)
239 |         val hasUninstall = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Uninstall").isNotEmpty()
240 |         val hasForceStop = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Force stop").isNotEmpty() || 
241 |                            root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("FORCE STOP").isNotEmpty()
242 |         
243 |         if (hasUninstall && hasForceStop) {
244 |             val hasAppName = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Eyecare").isNotEmpty()
245 |             if (hasAppName) {
246 |                  DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "App Info Detected for Eyecare. KILLING.")
247 |                  machineGun()
248 |                  startTripwire()
249 |                  return
250 |             }
251 | 
252 |             // Generic App Info Logic (Freeze)
253 |             if (!verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
254 |                 val isSafe = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Permissions").isNotEmpty() || 
255 |                              root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText("Notifications").isNotEmpty()
256 |                 if (isSafe) {
257 |                     verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = true
258 |                 } else {
259 |                     if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_VIEW_CLICKED) {
260 |                         DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "Freeze Violation: Interaction on unverified page.")
261 |                         machineGun()
262 |                     }
263 |                 }
264 |             }
265 |         }
266 | 
267 |         // C. DIALOG TRAP
268 |         if (className.contains("Dialog") || className.contains("popup", ignoreCase = true)) {
269 |              val sb = StringBuilder()
270 |              collectTextRecursive(root, sb)
271 |              val text = sb.toString()
272 |              if (text.contains("Eyecare", ignoreCase = true) && 
273 |                 (text.contains("Stop", ignoreCase = true) || text.contains("Turn off", ignoreCase = true))) {
274 |                  DebugLogger.log("SECURITY", "Dialog Trap: Destructive text detected.")
275 |                  machineGun()
276 |              }
277 |         }
278 |     }

280 |     // --- TACTICAL SHIELD IMPLEMENTATION ---
281 |     
282 |     private fun showShield() {
283 |         if (shieldView != null) return // Already UP
284 |         if (!android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(this)) return
285 | 
286 |         try {
287 |             val params = WindowManager.LayoutParams(
288 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
289 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
290 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY,
291 |                 // FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE = Allow key events to pass behind (like Back btn), 
292 |                 // BUT we do NOT set FLAG_NOT_TOUCH_MODAL. This means we CONSUME touches.
293 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE or 
294 |                 WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_LAYOUT_IN_SCREEN,
295 |                 PixelFormat.TRANSLUCENT
296 |             )
297 |             params.gravity = Gravity.CENTER
298 | 
299 |             shieldView = FrameLayout(this).apply {
300 |                 setBackgroundColor(Color.TRANSPARENT) // Invisible
301 |                 // Optional: setBackgroundColor(Color.parseColor("#22FF0000")) // Debug Red Tint
302 |             }
303 | 
304 |             windowManager?.addView(shieldView, params)
305 |             // DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Visual Shield Deployed")
306 | 
307 |             // AUTO-DISMISS TIMER (2s MAX)
308 |             shieldJob?.cancel()
309 |             shieldJob = scope.launch {
310 |                 delay(2000)
311 |                 if (guardianState == GuardianState.SCANNING) {
312 |                     DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Timeout: No anchors found. Dropping shield.")
313 |                     hideShield()
314 |                     guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
315 |                 }
316 |             }
317 | 
318 |         } catch (e: Exception) {
319 |             DebugLogger.log("ERROR", "Failed to deploy shield: \${e.message}")
320 |         }
321 |     }
322 | 
323 |     private fun hideShield() {
324 |         if (shieldView == null) return
325 |         try {
326 |             windowManager?.removeView(shieldView)
327 |             shieldView = null
328 |             // DebugLogger.log("SHIELD", "Shield Retracted")
329 |         } catch (e: Exception) {
330 |             shieldView = null
331 |         }
332 |     }

334 |     // --- UTILS ---
335 | 
336 |     private fun collectText(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, list: MutableList<String>) {
337 |         if (node == null) return
338 |         if (node.text != null && node.text.isNotEmpty()) list.add(node.text.toString())
339 |         if (node.contentDescription != null && node.contentDescription.isNotEmpty()) list.add(node.contentDescription.toString())
340 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
341 |             collectText(node.getChild(i), list)
342 |         }
343 |     }
344 | 
345 |     private fun collectTextRecursive(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, sb: StringBuilder) {
346 |         if (node == null) return
347 |         if (node.text != null) sb.append(node.text).append(" ")
348 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
349 |             collectTextRecursive(node.getChild(i), sb)
350 |         }
351 |     }
352 | 
353 |     private fun findSwitch(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?): AccessibilityNodeInfo? {
354 |         if (node == null) return null
355 |         if (node.className == "android.widget.Switch") return node
356 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
357 |             val result = findSwitch(node.getChild(i))
358 |             if (result != null) return result
359 |         }
360 |         return null
361 |     }
362 | 
363 |     private fun machineGun() {
364 |         guardianState = GuardianState.FIRING
365 |         hideShield() // Drop shield so back button works
366 |         DebugLogger.log("COMBAT", "!!! MACHINE GUN FIRED !!!")
367 |         repeat(4) {
368 |             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_BACK)
369 |         }
370 |         // Reset state after firing sequence
371 |         scope.launch {
372 |              delay(1000)
373 |              guardianState = GuardianState.IDLE
374 |         }
375 |     }

378 |         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
379 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
380 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
381 |         i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", "SECURITY_TRIPWIRE")

385 |     private fun runGuardianLoop() {
386 |         timerJob = scope.launch {
387 |             while (isActive) {
388 |                 if (isScreenActive) {
389 |                     TimeManager.checkNewDay(this@EyeCareService)
390 | 
391 |                     if (!android.provider.Settings.canDrawOverlays(applicationContext)) {
392 |                          val i = Intent(android.provider.Settings.ACTION_MANAGE_OVERLAY_PERMISSION)
393 |                          i.data = android.net.Uri.parse("package:$packageName")
394 |                          i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
395 |                          startActivity(i)

398 |                     if (TimeManager.isBreakActive) {
399 |                         val pkg = currentPackage
400 |                         val isSafe = isCriticalAppForeground()

403 |                             val i = Intent(applicationContext, BreakActivity::class.java)
404 |                             i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
405 |                             i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT)
406 |                             i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
407 |                             startActivity(i)
408 |                         }
409 |                     }
410 | 
411 |                     updateUsage()
412 |                     checkRules()
413 |                     StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
414 | 
415 |                     if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs % 60_000L < 2500) {
416 |                         TimeManager.save(this@EyeCareService)
417 |                     }
418 |                     delay(1000)

339 |         if (node.contentDescription != null && node.contentDescription.isNotEmpty()) list.add(node.contentDescription.toString())

348 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
349 |             collectTextRecursive(node.getChild(i), sb)

357 |             val result = findSwitch(node.getChild(i))
358 |             if (result != null) return result
359 |         }
360 |         return null
361 |     }

415 |                     updateUsage()

417 |                     StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
418 | 
419 |                     if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs % 60_000L < 2500) {

421 |                     }
422 |                     delay(1000)

430 |     private fun isCriticalAppForeground(): Boolean {
431 |         val safeApps = setOf(
432 |             "com.google.android.apps.maps", "com.waze", 
433 |             "com.google.android.dialer", "com.android.phone", "com.android.server.telecom",
434 |             "com.google.android.deskclock", "com.sec.android.app.clockpackage", "com.android.deskclock"
435 |         )
436 |         val lowerPkg = currentPackage.lowercase()
437 |         if (safeApps.contains(currentPackage)) return true
438 |         return lowerPkg.contains("dialer") || lowerPkg.contains("call") || 
439 |                lowerPkg.contains("clock") || lowerPkg.contains("alarm")
440 |     }
441 | 
442 |     private fun updateUsage() {
443 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
444 |         val diff = now - TimeManager.sessionStart
445 |         TimeManager.sessionStart = now
446 |         TimeManager.continuousUsage += diff
447 |         TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs += diff
448 |     }
449 | 
450 |     private suspend fun checkRules() {
451 |         if (isCriticalAppForeground()) {
452 |              hideBanner()
453 |              return
454 |         }
455 | 
456 |         val h = java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
457 |         val isNightShift = (h >= 23 || h < 5)
458 |         if (isNightShift && TimeManager.isNightPassScheduled) {
459 |              hideBanner()
460 |              return 
461 |         }
462 | 
463 |         if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs >= TimeManager.DAILY_CAP) {
464 |              triggerBreak("DAILY_LIMIT", 0)
465 |              return
466 |         }
467 | 
468 |         val remainingLockout = TimeManager.getLockoutRemaining(this)
469 |         if (remainingLockout > 0) {
470 |              triggerBreak("MANUAL_LOCKOUT", remainingLockout)
471 |              return
472 |         }
473 | 
474 |         if (System.currentTimeMillis() < TimeManager.snoozeUntil) {
475 |              hideBanner()
476 |              return
477 |         }
478 | 
479 |         val usage = TimeManager.continuousUsage
480 | 
481 |         if (isNightShift) {
482 |              hideBanner()
483 |              triggerBreak("SLEEP_MODE", 0)
484 |              return
485 |         }
486 |         
487 |         if (TimeManager.nextLongBreak - usage <= 0) {
488 |              triggerBreak("EXTENDED", 50 * 60 * 1000L)
489 |              TimeManager.nextLongBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_2_HOUR
490 |              return
491 |         }
492 |         if (TimeManager.nextMediumBreak - usage <= 0) {
493 |              triggerBreak("LONG", 20 * 60 * 1000L)
494 |              TimeManager.nextMediumBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_60_MIN
495 |              return
496 |         }
497 |         if (TimeManager.nextShortBreak - usage <= 0) {
498 |              triggerBreak("SHORT", 300 * 1000L)
499 |              TimeManager.nextShortBreak += TimeManager.INTERVAL_30_MIN
500 |              return
501 |         }
502 |     }
503 | 
504 |     private fun triggerBreak(type: String, duration: Long) {
505 |         if (currentPackage == packageName && TimeManager.isBreakActive) return
506 |         val i = Intent(this, BreakActivity::class.java)
507 |         i.putExtra("TYPE", type)
508 |         i.putExtra("DURATION", duration)
509 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT)
510 |         startActivity(i)
511 |     }

513 |     private fun hideBanner() {
514 |         try {
515 |             if (warningView != null) {
516 |                 val container = warningView?.tag as? View
517 |                 if (container != null && container.parent != null) {
518 |                     windowManager?.removeView(container)
519 |                 }
520 |                 warningView = null
521 |             }
522 |         } catch (e: Exception) { }
523 |     }

TASK 1: Write a DETAILED technical summary of why the build failed.

The build failed during the Kotlin compilation phase for the `debug` build variant, specifically during the execution of the `:app:compileDebugKotlin` Gradle task.

**Error Details:**

1.  **Failing Task:** `:app:compileDebugKotlin`
2.  **Build Outcome:** `FAILED`
3.  **Error Message Snippet:**
    ```
    > Task :app:compileDebugKotlin FAILED
    e: file:///home/runner/work/Eyecare/Eyecare/app/src/main/java/com/eyecare/guard/EyeCareService.kt:461:54 Unresolved reference: View
    ```
4.  **Root Cause Explanation:** The Kotlin compiler encountered an `Unresolved reference: View` error on line 461, column 54, within the `EyeCareService.kt` file located in the `app/src/main/java/com/eyecare/guard/` directory. This type of error indicates that a necessary class or object reference (`View` in this case) was used without being imported or is otherwise unavailable in the current scope of the Kotlin source file. In an Android/Kotlin context, `View` typically needs to be imported from the Android SDK packages (e.g., `android.view.View`). The compilation process halted immediately upon encountering this critical error.

The build subsequently failed with exit code 1, and the cleanup processes noted several warnings related to failing to save Gradle cache entries, although these cache issues are secondary to the primary compilation failure.

TASK 2: Identify the specific file path(s) in the repo causing the error.

The error originates directly from the Kotlin source file being compiled.

ops.text: Sync Patcher Engine logic with new Healer strategies

messages_preview.content:
YOU ARE THE CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE.

### MISSION
A code patch failed because the 'find_block' or 'anchor' provided by the user did not match the file content EXACTLY. Your mission is to find where that code was INTENDED to go by performing high-level semantic and fuzzy matching.

### CORE MATCHING RULES
1. WHITESPACE INDEPENDENCE: Ignore all tabs, spaces, and newlines. If the logic is the same, it is a MATCH.
2. SEMANTIC EQUIVALENCE: 'obj.prop' is the same as 'obj . prop'. 'x=1' is the same as 'x = 1'.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target code is generic (like just a '}' or 'else'), look at the lines BEFORE and AFTER to confirm the location.
4. DEGRADATION TOLERANCE: If the user provided 5 lines of code, but the file only has 4 of them (and 1 changed slightly), treat it as a match on those lines.

### OUTPUT STRATEGIES (Choose ONE)
- STRATEGY A (Range Replace): Use 'start_line' and 'end_line' if you found the exact block. This is the most reliable.
- STRATEGY B (Line Insert): Use 'anchor_line' if the user wanted to 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' a line that moved.
- STRATEGY C (String Anchor): Use 'new_anchor_text' if you found a more unique string that the Patcher can use for a standard match.

### DATA INTEGRITY REQUIREMENTS
- confidence_score: MUST BE AN INTEGER BETWEEN 0 AND 100. DO NOT USE DECIMALS (e.g., Use 95, NOT 0.95).
- start_line / end_line: These are 1-based indices corresponding to the line numbers provided in the 'File' context.
- explanation: Be technical. Explain exactly what caused the mismatch (e.g., "User expected a single line, but the file split the arguments across lines 12-14").

### EXAMPLE SCENARIOS

Scenario 1: User wants to replace a function, but added a comment in the find_block that doesn't exist.
- Fix: Set 'start_line' and 'end_line' to the actual function boundaries in the file. Set score to 90.

Scenario 2: User wants to insert after 'const x = 10', but the file has 'const x=10' (no spaces).
- Fix: Set 'anchor_line' to the line number where 'const x=10' exists. Set score to 100.

Scenario 3: The code is nowhere to be found.
- Fix: Set 'can_fix' to false. Explain that the logic appears to have been deleted. Set score to 0.

Final Instruction: Look at the line numbers provided in the context carefully. If a block starts on line 10 and ends on line 15, 'start_line' is 10 and 'end_line' is 15.

 6 | object TimeManager {
 7 |     private const val PREFS = "time_prefs"
 8 |     
 9 |     // CONSTANTS
10 |     const val DAILY_CAP = 9 * 60 * 60 * 1000L // 9 Hours
11 |     const val RESET_WINDOW = 15 * 60 * 1000L // 15 Minutes screen off to reset continuous
12 |     
13 |     const val INTERVAL_30_MIN = 30 * 60 * 1000L
14 |     const val INTERVAL_60_MIN = 60 * 60 * 1000L
15 |     const val INTERVAL_2_HOUR = 120 * 60 * 1000L
16 | 
17 |     // STATE
18 |     var sessionStart: Long = System.currentTimeMillis()
19 |     var continuousUsage: Long = 0L
20 |     var accumulatedDailyMs: Long = 0L
21 |     var lastScreenOffTs: Long = 0L
22 |     var breakEndTs: Long = 0L
23 |     var manualLockoutEndTs: Long = 0L
24 |     var isNightPassScheduled: Boolean = false
25 |     var lastDayOfYear: Int = -1
26 | 
27 |     fun init(ctx: Context) {
28 |         val prefs = ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
29 |         accumulatedDailyMs = prefs.getLong("daily_ms", 0L)
30 |         continuousUsage = prefs.getLong("cont_ms", 0L)
31 |         lastScreenOffTs = prefs.getLong("off_ts", 0L)
32 |         lastDayOfYear = prefs.getInt("last_day", -1)
33 |         isNightPassScheduled = prefs.getBoolean("night_pass", false)
34 |         manualLockoutEndTs = prefs.getLong("manual_lock_ts", 0L)
35 |     }
36 | 
37 |     fun save(ctx: Context) {
38 |         ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE).edit().apply {
39 |             putLong("daily_ms", accumulatedDailyMs)
40 |             putLong("cont_ms", continuousUsage)
41 |             putLong("off_ts", lastScreenOffTs)
42 |             putInt("last_day", lastDayOfYear)
43 |             putBoolean("night_pass", isNightPassScheduled)
44 |             putLong("manual_lock_ts", manualLockoutEndTs)
45 |             apply()
46 |         }
47 |     }
48 | 
49 |     fun setManualLockout(ctx: Context, minutes: Int) {
50 |         manualLockoutEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (minutes * 60 * 1000L)
51 |         save(ctx)
52 |     }
53 | 
54 |     fun checkNewDay(ctx: Context) {
55 |         val currentDay = Calendar.getInstance().get(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR)
56 |         if (lastDayOfYear != -1 && lastDayOfYear != currentDay) {
57 |             accumulatedDailyMs = 0
58 |             continuousUsage = 0
59 |             isNightPassScheduled = false
60 |             save(ctx)
61 |         }
62 |         lastDayOfYear = currentDay
63 |     }
64 | 
65 |     fun resetContinuous(ctx: Context) {
66 |         continuousUsage = 0
67 |         save(ctx)
68 |     }
69 | 
70 |     fun isBreakActive(): Boolean {
71 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
72 |         if (now < manualLockoutEndTs) return true
73 |         if (now < breakEndTs) return true
74 |         if (accumulatedDailyMs >= DAILY_CAP) return true
75 |         
76 |         // Sleep Mode Check
77 |         val h = Calendar.getInstance().get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
78 |         val isNight = (h >= 23 || h < 5)
79 |         if (isNight && !isNightPassScheduled) return true
80 |         
81 |         return false
82 |     }
83 | }

const systemInstruction = `### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- If you are replacing a block that spans multiple lines, YOU MUST PROVIDE BOTH 'start_line' AND 'end_line'. NEVER provide a start_line alone for a multi-line replacement.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

data.analysis:
The log indicates a **build failure** during the compilation of Kotlin code (`:app:compileDebugKotlin`).

Here is the specific error analysis:

### Specific Error

The primary errors are compilation errors from the Kotlin compiler, characterized by **"Overload resolution ambiguity"** and **"Unresolved reference"**.

1.  **Location & Error Type (Overload Ambiguity):**
    *   **File/Line:** `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/GuardService.kt:388:53`
    *   **Error:** `Overload resolution ambiguity: public final var isNightPassScheduled: Boolean defined in com.guardian.net.TimeManager` (This error repeats multiple times in different files).

2.  **Location & Error Type (Unresolved Reference):**
    *   **File/Line:** `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/MainActivity.kt:114:18`
    *   **Error:** `Unresolved reference: verticalScroll`

3.  **Location & Error Type (Conflicting Declarations):**
    *   **File/Line:** `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/TimeManager.kt:24:9`
    *   **Error:** `Conflicting declarations: public final var isNightPassScheduled: Boolean, public final var isNightPassScheduled: Boolean`

### Brief Summary

The **Kotlin compilation failed** due to several related issues within the code:

1.  **Ambiguity/Conflict in `TimeManager.kt`:** The property `isNightPassScheduled` is being declared or accessed ambiguously or conflictingly within `TimeManager.kt` itself, and also in files referencing it (`GuardService.kt`, `MainActivity.kt`). This suggests an issue with how properties are defined (perhaps duplicated definitions, or visibility issues if the property is being accessed from different contexts that lead to multiple resolution paths).
2.  **Missing Reference in `MainActivity.kt`:** The identifier `verticalScroll` is not recognized at line 114 in `MainActivity.kt`. This function/property likely needs to be imported or qualified correctly, especially if using Jetpack Compose, where modifiers need explicit imports.

### Secondary Log Observation (Non-Fatal Warnings)

*   **Manifest Warning:** The build process noted that defining the `package` attribute directly in `app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` is deprecated and ignored. (Recommendation: Remove `package="com.guardian.net"` from the source manifest).
*   **Cache Failures:** The build failed to save several Gradle cache entries (`gradle-home-v1`, dependency caches, etc.) due to a service error from the GitHub Actions caching service ("Our services aren't available right now"). **This did not cause the build failure, but it means subsequent builds might be slightly slower as they won't restore the full cache.**

ops.text: Fix TimeManager conflicts, resolve verticalScroll import, and clean manifest

ops.content:
package com.guardian.net

import android.content.Context
import java.util.Calendar

object TimeManager {
    private const val PREFS = "time_prefs"
    
    const val DAILY_CAP = 9 * 60 * 60 * 1000L
    const val RESET_WINDOW = 15 * 60 * 1000L
    
    const val INTERVAL_30_MIN = 30 * 60 * 1000L
    const val INTERVAL_60_MIN = 60 * 60 * 1000L
    const val INTERVAL_2_HOUR = 120 * 60 * 1000L

    var sessionStart: Long = System.currentTimeMillis()
    var continuousUsage: Long = 0L
    var accumulatedDailyMs: Long = 0L
    var lastScreenOffTs: Long = 0L
    var breakEndTs: Long = 0L
    var manualLockoutEndTs: Long = 0L
    var isNightPassScheduled: Boolean = false
    var nightPassUsedThisWeek: Int = 0
    var lastDayOfYear: Int = -1
    var lastWeekOfYear: Int = -1

    fun init(ctx: Context) {
        val prefs = ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
        accumulatedDailyMs = prefs.getLong("daily_ms", 0L)
        continuousUsage = prefs.getLong("cont_ms", 0L)
        lastScreenOffTs = prefs.getLong("off_ts", 0L)
        breakEndTs = prefs.getLong("break_end_ts", 0L)
        manualLockoutEndTs = prefs.getLong("manual_lock_ts", 0L)
        isNightPassScheduled = prefs.getBoolean("night_pass", false)
        nightPassUsedThisWeek = prefs.getInt("night_pass_count", 0)
        lastDayOfYear = prefs.getInt("last_day", -1)
        lastWeekOfYear = prefs.getInt("last_week", -1)
    }

    fun save(ctx: Context) {
        ctx.getSharedPreferences(PREFS, Context.MODE_PRIVATE).edit().apply {
            putLong("daily_ms", accumulatedDailyMs)
            putLong("cont_ms", continuousUsage)
            putLong("off_ts", lastScreenOffTs)
            putLong("break_end_ts", breakEndTs)
            putLong("manual_lock_ts", manualLockoutEndTs)
            putBoolean("night_pass", isNightPassScheduled)
            putInt("night_pass_count", nightPassUsedThisWeek)
            putInt("last_day", lastDayOfYear)
            putInt("last_week", lastWeekOfYear)
            apply()
        }
    }

    fun checkNewDay(ctx: Context) {
        val cal = Calendar.getInstance()
        val currentDay = cal.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR)
        val currentWeek = cal.get(Calendar.WEEK_OF_YEAR)

        var changed = false
        if (lastDayOfYear != -1 && lastDayOfYear != currentDay) {
            accumulatedDailyMs = 0
            continuousUsage = 0
            isNightPassScheduled = false
            changed = true
        }
        if (lastWeekOfYear != -1 && lastWeekOfYear != currentWeek) {
            nightPassUsedThisWeek = 0
            changed = true
        }

        lastDayOfYear = currentDay
        lastWeekOfYear = currentWeek
        if (changed) save(ctx)
    }

    fun resetContinuous(ctx: Context) {
        continuousUsage = 0
        save(ctx)
    }

    fun setManualLockout(ctx: Context, minutes: Int) {
        manualLockoutEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (minutes * 60 * 1000L)
        save(ctx)
    }

    fun isBreakActive(): Boolean {
        val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
        if (now < manualLockoutEndTs) return true
        if (now < breakEndTs) return true
        if (accumulatedDailyMs >= DAILY_CAP) return true
        
        val h = Calendar.getInstance().get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
        val isNight = (h >= 23 || h < 5)
        if (isNight && !isNightPassScheduled) return true
        
        return false
    }
}

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.

- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.

const textbook = [
        {
            "id": "ch3_intro",
            "unit": "ETHIOPIA & THE HORN",
            "subtitle": "CHAPTER 3: THE TOPOGRAPHY OF ETHIOPIA. The topography is largely determined by the geologic activities of the Cenozoic Era. The uplifting of the Arabo-Ethiopian swell and Trapean lava have given rise to an outward sloping highland plateau.",
            "camera": { "center": [39.5, 9.0], "zoom": 5.5, "pitch": 30, "bearing": 0 },

ops.content:
package com.guardian.net

import android.service.quicksettings.Tile
import android.service.quicksettings.TileService
import android.widget.Toast

class DevKillTileService : TileService() {
    override fun onStartListening() {
        super.onStartListening()
        val killed = DevSettings.isKilled(applicationContext)
        qsTile.state = if (killed) Tile.STATE_ACTIVE else Tile.STATE_INACTIVE
        qsTile.label = if (killed) "Guardian: KILLED" else "Guardian: ON"
        qsTile.updateTile()
    }

    override fun onClick() {
        super.onClick()
        val newState = !DevSettings.isKilled(applicationContext)
        DevSettings.setKilled(applicationContext, newState)
        qsTile.state = if (newState) Tile.STATE_ACTIVE else Tile.STATE_INACTIVE
        qsTile.label = if (newState) "Guardian: KILLED" else "Guardian: ON"
        qsTile.updateTile()
        val msg = if (newState) "Dev Kill Active" else "Guardian Restored"
        Toast.makeText(this, msg, Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show()
    }
}

messages_preview.content:
### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).
- 0: No logical match exists.

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").
- 'can_fix': If you are guessing with less than 60% confidence, set this to false.

  1 | package com.guardian.net
  2 | 
  3 | import android.accessibilityservice.AccessibilityService
  4 | import android.content.Intent
  5 | import android.net.Uri
  6 | import android.provider.Settings
  7 | import android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityEvent
  8 | import android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityNodeInfo
  9 | import kotlinx.coroutines.*
 10 | 
 11 | class GuardService : AccessibilityService() {
 12 | 
 13 |     private val job = SupervisorJob()
 14 |     private val scope = CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.Default + job)
 15 | 
 16 |     // POLLING & STRIKE SYSTEM
 17 |     private var pollingJob: Job? = null
 18 |     private var activePackage = ""
 19 |     private val browserStrikes = mutableListOf<Long>()
 20 |     private val telegramStrikes = mutableListOf<Long>()
 21 |     
 22 |     // COOLDOWN: Prevents 100 strikes in 1 second
 23 |     private var lastBrowserAction = 0L
 24 |     private var lastTelegramAction = 0L
 25 | 
 26 |     // TIMESTAMP: Tracks when you were last touching settings
 27 |     private var lastSettingsInteraction: Long = 0L
 28 |     
 29 |     // SYNC: Tracks last time we pulled updates from Supabase
 30 |     private var lastCloudSync: Long = 0L
 31 |     // SESSION: Remembers if the current App Info page has been proven innocent
 32 |     private var verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = false
 33 |     private var isScreenActive = true
 34 | 
 35 |     // DYNAMIC LEARNING: Remembers any app that has shown a WebView during this session
 36 |     private val dynamicBrowsers = mutableSetOf<String>()
 37 | 
 38 |     private val screenReceiver = object : android.content.BroadcastReceiver() {
 39 |         override fun onReceive(context: android.content.Context, intent: android.content.Intent) {
 40 |             if (intent.action == android.content.Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF) {
 41 |                 isScreenActive = false
 42 |                 TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs = System.currentTimeMillis()
 43 |                 TimeManager.save(context)
 44 |             } else if (intent.action == android.content.Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON) {
 45 |                 isScreenActive = true
 46 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
 47 |                 if (TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs > 0 && (now - TimeManager.lastScreenOffTs) > TimeManager.RESET_WINDOW) {
 48 |                     TimeManager.resetContinuous(context)
 49 |                 }
 50 |                 TimeManager.sessionStart = now
 51 |             }
 52 |         }
 53 |     }
 54 | 
 55 |         override fun onServiceConnected() {
 56 |         super.onServiceConnected()    
 57 |         StatsManager.init(this)
 58 |         TimeManager.init(this)
 59 |         PermissionTracker.setEverEnabled(this)
 60 |         PermissionTracker.setPenaltyEnd(this, 0L)
 61 |         PermissionTracker.clearFixWindow(this)
 62 | 
 63 |         val filter = android.content.IntentFilter().apply {
 64 |             addAction(android.content.Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON)
 65 |             addAction(android.content.Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF)
 66 |         }
 67 |         registerReceiver(screenReceiver, filter)
 68 |         
 69 |         // ELEVATE PRIORITY: Persistent Notification
 70 |         startForegroundService()
 71 |         startMonitoring()
 72 |     }
 73 | 
 74 |     private fun startForegroundService() {
 75 |         val channelId = "dns_guard_channel"
 76 |         val nm = getSystemService(android.app.NotificationManager::class.java)
 77 |         if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
 78 |             val chan = android.app.NotificationChannel(
 79 |                 channelId, 
 80 |                 "DNS Monitor", 
 81 |                 android.app.NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_MIN // Minimal intrusion
 82 |             )
 83 |             nm.createNotificationChannel(chan)
 84 |         }
 85 | 
 86 |         val notif = androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.Builder(this, channelId)
 87 |             .setContentTitle("Protection Active")
 88 |             .setContentText("Guardian is monitoring network security.")
 89 |             .setSmallIcon(android.R.drawable.ic_secure)
 90 |             .setPriority(androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_MIN)
 91 |             .setOngoing(true)
 92 |             .build()
 93 |         
 94 |         // 1337 is the notification ID
 95 |         startForeground(1337, notif)
 96 |     }
 97 | 
 98 |     override fun onAccessibilityEvent(event: AccessibilityEvent?) {
 99 |         if (event == null) return
100 |         val pkg = event.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
101 |         // FIX: Session Leaking. Reset verification when window state changes (e.g. Recents/Alt-Tab).
102 |         if (event.eventType == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGED) {

104 |         }

106 | 
107 |         // GLOBAL RESET: If we switch to a different app (ignore SystemUI overlays like volume/keyboard)
108 |         if (!pkg.contains("settings") && !pkg.contains("packageinstaller") && 
109 |             !pkg.contains("accessibility") && !pkg.contains("systemui")) {
110 |             if (verifiedSafeAppInfoSession) {
111 |                 DebugLogger.log("Reset", "Left Settings (Pkg: $pkg). Session Invalidated.")
112 |                 verifiedSafeAppInfoSession = false

114 |         }

116 |         // Update active package and manage heartbeat polling
117 |         if (pkg != activePackage) {
118 |             activePackage = pkg

122 |         // LEARN: If this app displays a WebView, mark it as a browser for the session
123 |         if (event.className == "android.webkit.WebView") {
124 |             if (dynamicBrowsers.add(pkg)) {
125 |                 DebugLogger.log("LEARN", "Detected hidden WebView in $pkg. Marked as browser.")

154 |         // If penalty box is active, block access immediately.

156 |         if (isBrowserCheck && LockManager.isBrowserBanned(applicationContext)) {

354 |     private fun updateUsage() {
355 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
356 |         val diff = now - TimeManager.sessionStart
357 |         TimeManager.sessionStart = now
358 |         
359 |         if (diff > 0 && diff < 10000) {
360 |             TimeManager.continuousUsage += diff
361 |             TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs += diff
362 | 
363 |             // DEBT TRACKER: If using phone during Sleep Protocol hours
364 |             val h = java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
365 |             if ((h >= 23 || h < 5) && TimeManager.isNightPassScheduled) {
366 |                 TimeManager.nightDebtMs += diff
367 |             }
368 |         }
369 |         if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs % 60000 < 2000) TimeManager.save(applicationContext)
370 |     }

372 |     private fun checkTemporalRules() {
373 |         if (isCriticalAppForeground()) return
374 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
375 | 
376 |         if (TimeManager.isBreakActive()) {
377 |             if (activePackage != packageName) triggerTemporalLock()
378 |             return
379 |         }
380 | 
381 |         if (now < TimeManager.manualLockoutEndTs) {
382 |             if (activePackage != packageName) triggerTemporalLock("MANUAL_LOCKOUT")
383 |             return
384 |         }
385 | 
386 |         val usage = TimeManager.continuousUsage
387 |         if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs >= TimeManager.DAILY_CAP) {
388 |              triggerTemporalLock("DAILY_LIMIT")
389 |         } else {
390 |              val h = java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
391 |              if ((h >= 23 || h < 5) && !TimeManager.isNightPassScheduled) {
392 |                  triggerTemporalLock("SLEEP_MODE")
393 |              } else if (usage >= TimeManager.INTERVAL_2_HOUR) {
394 |                  TimeManager.breakEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (50 * 60 * 1000L)
395 |                  TimeManager.resetContinuous(applicationContext)
396 |                  triggerTemporalLock("BREAK")
397 |              } else if (usage >= TimeManager.INTERVAL_60_MIN) {
398 |                  TimeManager.breakEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (20 * 60 * 1000L)
399 |                  TimeManager.resetContinuous(applicationContext)
400 |                  triggerTemporalLock("BREAK")
401 |              } else if (usage >= TimeManager.INTERVAL_30_MIN) {
402 |                  TimeManager.breakEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (5 * 60 * 1000L)
403 |                  TimeManager.resetContinuous(applicationContext)
404 |                  triggerTemporalLock("BREAK")
405 |              }
406 |         }
407 |     }
408 | 
409 |     private fun triggerTemporalLock(type: String? = null) {
410 |         val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
411 |         val finalType = type ?: if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs >= TimeManager.DAILY_CAP) "DAILY_LIMIT" 
412 |                         else if (java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY) >= 23 || java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY) < 5) "SLEEP_MODE" 
413 |                         else "BREAK"
414 |         
415 |         i.putExtra("BLOCK_TYPE", finalType)
416 |         i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NO_ANIMATION)
417 |         startActivity(i)
418 |     }

421 |         return isCriticalAppPackage(activePackage)

424 |     private fun isCriticalAppPackage(pkg: String): Boolean {
425 |         val safeApps = setOf(
426 |             "com.google.android.apps.maps", "com.waze", 
427 |             "com.google.android.dialer", "com.samsung.android.dialer", "com.android.phone", "com.android.server.telecom",
428 |             "com.google.android.deskclock", "com.sec.android.app.clockpackage", "com.android.deskclock"
429 |         )
430 |         val lowerPkg = pkg.lowercase()
431 |         return safeApps.contains(pkg) || 
432 |                lowerPkg.contains("dialer") || lowerPkg.contains("call") || 
433 |                lowerPkg.contains("clock") || lowerPkg.contains("alarm")
434 |     }

436 |     // HELPER: Recursive scan for the Dialog Trap
437 |     private fun recursiveScan(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo?, sb: StringBuilder) {
438 |         if (node == null) return
439 |         if (node.text != null) sb.append(node.text).append(" ")
440 |         if (node.contentDescription != null) sb.append(node.contentDescription).append(" ")
441 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
442 |             recursiveScan(node.getChild(i), sb)
443 |         }
444 |     }
