448 |     private fun startMonitoring() {
449 |         scope.launch {
450 |             while (isActive) {
451 |                 if (DevSettings.isKilled(applicationContext)) {
452 |                     stopForeground(STOP_FOREGROUND_REMOVE)
453 |                     delay(5000)
454 |                     continue
455 |                 }
456 | 
457 |                 if (isScreenActive) {
458 |                     TimeManager.checkNewDay(applicationContext)
459 |                     updateUsage()
460 |                     checkTemporalRules()
461 |                 }
462 | 
463 |                 if (isCriticalAppForeground()) {
464 |                     delay(2000)
465 |                     continue
466 |                 }
467 | 
468 |                 if (TimeManager.isBreakActive()) {
469 |                     val wins = windows
470 |                     var exploit = false
471 |                     for (w in wins) {
472 |                         val root = w.root ?: continue
473 |                         val p = root.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
474 |                         if (p != packageName && !p.contains("systemui") && !isCriticalAppPackage(p)) {
475 |                             exploit = true; break
476 |                         }
477 |                     }
478 |                     // Ensure we don't yank focus if a critical app is foreground
479 |                     if ((exploit || activePackage != packageName) && !isCriticalAppForeground()) {
480 |                         triggerTemporalLock()
481 |                     }
482 |                 }
483 | 
484 |                 NukeManager.checkAutoReEnable(applicationContext)
485 |                 NukeManager.checkNotifications(applicationContext)
486 | 
487 |                 if (LockManager.isUnlocked(applicationContext)) {
488 |                     delay(2000)
489 |                     continue
490 |                 }
491 | 
492 |                 if (NukeManager.isProtectionDisabled(applicationContext)) {
493 |                     delay(5000)
494 |                     continue
495 |                 }
496 | 
497 |                 if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lastSettingsInteraction < 2000) {
498 |                     delay(500)
499 |                     continue
500 |                 }
501 | 
502 |                 if (!Settings.canDrawOverlays(applicationContext)) {
503 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_MANAGE_OVERLAY_PERMISSION)
504 |                     i.data = Uri.parse("package:$packageName")
505 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
506 |                     startActivity(i)
507 |                 } else if ((Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME, 0) != 1 ||
508 |                           Settings.Global.getInt(contentResolver, Settings.Global.AUTO_TIME_ZONE, 0) != 1)) {
509 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_DATE_SETTINGS)
510 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
511 |                     startActivity(i)
512 |                 } else if (java.util.Locale.getDefault().language != "en") {
513 |                     val i = Intent(Settings.ACTION_LOCALE_SETTINGS)
514 |                     i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
515 |                     startActivity(i)
516 |                 } else if (!DnsManager.isSecure(applicationContext)) {
517 |                     triggerTemporalLock("DNS")
518 |                 }
519 | 
520 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
521 |                 if (now - lastCloudSync > 60 * 60 * 1000) {
522 |                     lastCloudSync = now
523 |                     scope.launch { CloudLogger.syncToCloud(applicationContext) }
524 |                 }
525 | 
526 |                 StatsManager.update(applicationContext)
527 |                 val pm = getSystemService(android.os.PowerManager::class.java)
528 |                 delay(if (pm.isInteractive) 2000 else 10000)
529 |             }
530 |         }
531 |     }

533 |     private fun managePolling(pkg: String) {
534 |         // Stop existing poll to avoid duplicates
535 |         pollingJob?.cancel()
536 | 
537 |         // FIX: Explicitly include Chrome & Google App for monitoring
538 |         val isBrowser = LockManager.isBlacklistedBrowser(applicationContext, pkg) || 
539 |                        pkg == "com.android.chrome" || 
540 |                        pkg == "com.google.android.googlequicksearchbox" ||
541 |                        dynamicBrowsers.contains(pkg)
542 | 
543 |         val isTelegram = pkg.contains("telegram") || pkg.contains("challegram")
544 | 
545 |         if (isBrowser || isTelegram) {
546 |             pollingJob = scope.launch {
547 |                 while (isActive) {
548 |                     delay(1500) // 1.5 Second Heartbeat
549 |                     scanForViolations(isBrowser, isTelegram)
550 |                 }
551 |             }
552 |         }
553 |     }

555 |     private fun scanForViolations(isBrowser: Boolean, isTelegram: Boolean) {
556 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
557 | 
558 |         // 1. BROWSER LOGIC (Strict Domain Matching)
559 |         if (isBrowser) {
560 |             // DEBUG SPY: Independent scan to capture URL bar IDs.
561 |             // Triggers if you type "google" or "http" to help you find the ID.
562 |             val spyKeywords = listOf("google", "http")
563 |             for (key in spyKeywords) {
564 |                 val spies = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(key)
565 |                 for (node in spies) {
566 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: "null"
567 |                     DebugLogger.log("SPY", "Pkg: $activePackage | ID: $resId | Text: ${node.text}")
568 |                 }
569 |             }
570 | 
571 |             val blacklist = listOf(
572 |                 // Social & Microblogging
573 |                 "bsky.app", "twitter.com", "x.com", "reddit.com", "tumblr.com", "threads.net", "plurk.com", "hive.social",
574 |                 // Fediverse
575 |                 "mastodon.social", "pawoo.net", "misskey.io", "pleroma.site", "lemmy.world", "truthsocial.com", "gab.com",
576 |                 // Community & Messaging
577 |                 "web.telegram.org", "t.me", "telegram.org", "discord.com", "kik.com", "snapchat.com", "slack.com",
578 |                 // Art & Creative
579 |                 "pixiv.net", "deviantart.com", "newgrounds.com", "artstation.com", "furaffinity.net", "hentai-foundry.com", "gelbooru.com", "danbooru.donmai.us",
580 |                 // Creator & Membership
581 |                 "onlyfans.com", "fansly.com", "patreon.com", "subscribestar.com", "fanbox.cc", "unifans.io", "buymeacoffee.com", "ko-fi.com",
582 |                 // Video & Streaming
583 |                 "kick.com", "bitchute.com", "rumble.com", "vimeo.com", "dailymotion.com", "dlive.tv", "picarto.tv",
584 |                 // Specialized & Existing
585 |                 "fetlife.com", "badoo.com", "tinder.com", "yubo.live", "instagram.com", "tiktok.com", "pornhub", "xnxx"
586 |             )
587 |             
588 |             for (site in blacklist) {
589 |                 val candidates = root.findAccessibilityNodeInfosByText(site)
590 |                 for (node in candidates) {
591 |                     // 1. CONTEXT CHECK: Is this the URL Bar?
592 |                     // We check if the node is editable (typing) OR if its ID indicates it's an address bar.
593 |                     val resId = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: ""
594 |                     
595 |                     // FIX: Broadened IDs to catch Firefox/Opera/Samsung 'Read-Only' URL bars
596 |                     val isUrlBar = node.isEditable || 
597 |                                    resId.contains("url") || 
598 |                                    resId.contains("address") || 
599 |                                    resId.contains("omnibox") || 
600 |                                    resId.contains("search_box") || 
601 |                                    resId.contains("location") ||
602 |                                    resId.contains("toolbar") ||
603 |                                    resId.contains("title") ||
604 |                                    resId.contains("input") ||
605 |                                    resId.contains("bar") ||
606 |                                    // COMPREHENSIVE LIST (Firefox, Bing, Samsung, etc)
607 |                                    resId.contains("mozac") ||
608 |                                    resId.contains("search_text") ||
609 |                                    resId.contains("edit_text") ||
610 |                                    resId.contains("query")
611 |                     // If it's just static text on a page (e.g. a Google Search Result description), ignore it.
612 |                     // However, if we are unsure, we err on the side of caution if the node is NOT a web content view.
613 |                     val isWebContent = resId.contains("content") || node.className == "android.webkit.WebView"
614 | 
615 |                     // HYBRID FALLBACK:
616 |                     // 1. If it's a URL bar (ID match), we scan it.
617 |                     // 2. If it's NOT a URL bar, we check if it's Web Content.
618 |                     //    - If it IS Web Content (and not a URL bar), we skip it (False Positive Protection).
619 |                     //    - If it is NOT Web Content (it's UI, like Firefox toolbar), we scan it.
620 |                     if (!isUrlBar && isWebContent) continue
621 | 
622 |                     
623 |                     // 2. TEXT MATCHING
624 |                     val rawText = (node.text?.toString() ?: "") + " " + (node.contentDescription?.toString() ?: "")
625 |                     val lowerText = rawText.lowercase()
626 |                     val index = lowerText.indexOf(site)
627 | 
628 |                     if (index != -1) {
629 |                         // STRICT CHECK: char before must NOT be letter/digit

### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).
- 0: No logical match exists.

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").
- 'can_fix': If you are guessing with less than 60% confidence, set this to false.

354 |     private fun updateUsage() {
355 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
356 |         val diff = now - TimeManager.sessionStart
357 |         TimeManager.sessionStart = now
358 |         
359 |         if (diff > 0 && diff < 10000) {
360 |             TimeManager.continuousUsage += diff
361 |             TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs += diff
362 | 
363 |             // DEBT TRACKER: If using phone during Sleep Protocol hours
364 |             val h = java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
365 |             if ((h >= 23 || h < 5) && TimeManager.isNightPassScheduled) {
366 |                 TimeManager.nightDebtMs += diff
367 |             }
368 |         }
369 |         if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs % 60000 < 2000) TimeManager.save(applicationContext)
370 |     }

372 |     private fun checkTemporalRules() {
373 |         if (isCriticalAppForeground()) return
374 |         val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
375 | 
376 |         if (TimeManager.isBreakActive()) {
377 |             if (activePackage != packageName) triggerTemporalLock()
378 |             return
379 |         }
380 | 
381 |         if (now < TimeManager.manualLockoutEndTs) {
382 |             if (activePackage != packageName) triggerTemporalLock("MANUAL_LOCKOUT")
383 |             return
384 |         }
385 | 
386 |         val usage = TimeManager.continuousUsage
387 |         if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs >= TimeManager.DAILY_CAP) {
388 |              triggerTemporalLock("DAILY_LIMIT")
389 |         } else {
390 |              val h = java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
391 |              if ((h >= 23 || h < 5) && !TimeManager.isNightPassScheduled) {
392 |                  triggerTemporalLock("SLEEP_MODE")
393 |              } else if (usage >= TimeManager.INTERVAL_2_HOUR) {
394 |                  TimeManager.breakEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (50 * 60 * 1000L)
395 |                  TimeManager.resetContinuous(applicationContext)
396 |                  triggerTemporalLock("BREAK")
397 |              } else if (usage >= TimeManager.INTERVAL_60_MIN) {
398 |                  TimeManager.breakEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (20 * 60 * 1000L)
399 |                  TimeManager.resetContinuous(applicationContext)
400 |                  triggerTemporalLock("BREAK")
401 |              } else if (usage >= TimeManager.INTERVAL_30_MIN) {
402 |                  TimeManager.breakEndTs = System.currentTimeMillis() + (5 * 60 * 1000L)
403 |                  TimeManager.resetContinuous(applicationContext)
404 |                  triggerTemporalLock("BREAK")
405 |              }
406 |         }
407 |     }

412 |                         else if (java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY) >= 23 || java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY) < 5) "SLEEP_MODE" 
413 |                         else "BREAK"

data.analysis:
The log indicates a **build failure** during the compilation step of the Android/Gradle project.

Here is the specific error, file/line, and a brief summary:

*   **Specific Error/Summary:** Compilation error in Kotlin files due to missing or unresolved references to Android Jetpack Compose/Animation APIs.
*   **Failing Task:** `:app:compileDebugKotlin`
*   **File/Lines with Errors:**
    *   `/home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/LockdownActivity.kt:75:38`: Unresolved reference: `rememberInfiniteTransition`
    *   `/home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/LockdownActivity.kt:78:33`, `78:52`, `78:65`: Unresolved references: `infiniteRepeatable`, `tween`, `RepeatMode`
    *   `/home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/LockdownActivity.kt:94:82`, `94:98`, `94:114`: Unresolved references: `graphicsLayer`, `scaleX`, `scaleY`
    *   `/home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/MainActivity.kt:293:49`, `299:48`: Unresolved reference: `background`
    *   `/home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/MainActivity.kt:515:23`: Unresolved reference: `LocalContext`

**Brief Summary:**

The build failed because the Kotlin compiler could not find several essential functions and properties (like `rememberInfiniteTransition`, `tween`, `graphicsLayer`, etc.) which strongly suggests that the **necessary Compose/Animation libraries (e.g., `androidx.compose.animation.*`) are missing or have incorrect versions specified in the project's Gradle dependencies.**

ops.text: Fix compilation errors: Add missing Compose Animation/Graphics dependencies and imports

ops.text: Optimize GuardService for Battery & Critical Apps, Fix Lockdown Logic

ops.find_block:
private fun triggerTemporalLock(type: String? = null) {
        val i = Intent(applicationContext, LockdownActivity::class.java)
        val finalType = type ?: if (TimeManager.accumulatedDailyMs >= TimeManager.DAILY_CAP) "DAILY_LIMIT" 
                        else if (java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY) >= 23 || java.util.Calendar.getInstance().get(java.util.Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY) < 5) "SLEEP_MODE"

data.analysis:
The log indicates a **build failure** during the compilation phase of the Android/Kotlin project.

Here is a summary of the critical error points:

### Specific Errors and Location

The build failed at the `:app:compileDebugKotlin` task due to compilation errors in Kotlin source files:

1.  **File:** `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/BreakOverlayManager.kt`
    *   **Line 50:** `Unresolved reference: isRestored`

2.  **File:** `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/LockdownActivity.kt`
    *   **Line 167:** `Unresolved reference: TextButton`
    *   **Line 168:** `@Composable invocations can only happen from the context of a @Composable function`
    *   **Line 190:** `Unresolved reference: TextButton` and `@Composable invocations can only happen from the context of a @Composable function` (repeated error related to composables)

3.  **File:** `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/MainActivity.kt`
    *   **Line 87 & 102:** `Cannot access 'showNotification': it is private in 'NukeManager'`

### Brief Summary

The pipeline successfully checked out the repository, set up Java 17, and initialized Gradle. However, the build failed at **`Task :app:compileDebugKotlin`** because of multiple **Kotlin compilation errors**, primarily involving:

1.  **Unresolved references** (`isRestored`, `TextButton`).
2.  **Incorrect usage of `@Composable` functions** (calling them outside a Composable context in `LockdownActivity.kt`).
3.  **Accessing private members** (`showNotification` in `NukeManager` from `MainActivity.kt`).

The process concluded with `BUILD FAILED in 3m 23s` and an exit code of 1.

### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).
- 0: No logical match exists.

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").
- 'can_fix': If you are guessing with less than 60% confidence, set this to false.

messages_preview.content:
### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).
- 0: No logical match exists.

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").
- 'can_fix': If you are guessing with less than 60% confidence, set this to false.

The log indicates a **build failure** during the compilation phase of the Android project.

Here is the specific error information:

*   **Specific Error:** `Execution failed for task ':app:compileDebugKotlin'.`
*   **File/Line:**
    *   `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/LockdownActivity.kt:167:25 Unresolved reference: TextButton`
    *   `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/LockdownActivity.kt:168:29 @Composable invocations can only happen from the context of a @Composable function`
    *   `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/MainActivity.kt:87:45 Cannot access 'showNotification': it is private in 'NukeManager'`
    *   `file:///home/runner/work/Addict/Addict/app/src/main/java/com/example/dnsguard/MainActivity.kt:102:41 Cannot access 'showNotification': it is private in 'NukeManager'`
*   **Brief Summary:** The Kotlin compilation failed due to several errors in two files:
    1.  **`LockdownActivity.kt`**: Errors suggest missing imports for Jetpack Compose components (`TextButton`) and incorrect usage of `@Composable` functions outside of a `@Composable` context.
    2.  **`MainActivity.kt`**: Errors indicate an attempt to access a `private` method (`showNotification`) within the `NukeManager` class from outside its scope.

The build process finished with `BUILD FAILED in 4m 15s` and an exit code of 1. There were also failures saving Gradle caches due to temporary service unavailability on the runner environment.

386 |         if (node.contentDescription != null) sb.append(node.contentDescription).append(" ")
